evals.prompt: Review this code: const user = await db.findById(req.params.id); return res.json(user);
evals.expected_output: Two violations: (1) A01/API1 — BOLA/IDOR: findById without owner filter. Fix: add AND owner_id = req.user.id to the query. (2) API3 — full entity returned, no DTO projection. Fix: return only the fields needed, never serialize raw ORM output. P0 finding — Security score capped at 40/100.

evals.prompt: I configured our CORS policy with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all routes including authenticated ones.
evals.expected_output: A05 — Security Misconfiguration (P0 if on authenticated routes). Wildcard CORS allows any origin to make credentialed requests. Fix: restrict to explicit allowlisted origins in an array. Authenticated routes must never use origin: '*'. Apply per-route CORS configuration.

evals.prompt: Write a login endpoint that checks the user's password.
evals.expected_output: Apply OWASP always-apply rules: (1) Hash passwords with Argon2id (never MD5/bcrypt). (2) Add rate limiting — no limit on auth is A04 Insecure Design. (3) Issue JWT with expiry — missing exp is A07. (4) Use HttpOnly cookie for token storage. (5) Return generic error ('Invalid credentials') for both wrong user and wrong password.

should_not_trigger: Write a React component for displaying products
Set up a development environment for Python
